user_prompt: Three-Plane Architecture for Controllable Agentic Software Engineering. A pyramid-structured diagram with three horizontal tiers. TOP TIER: Directive Plane (intent specification layer) - shows a brain/plan icon. An arrow labeled 'Intent Contract (IC)' flows DOWNWARD from this tier to the middle. Responsible for transforming human will into structured machine-executable intent. Serves outbound intent fidelity. MIDDLE TIER: Execution Plane (actuation layer) - shows a robotic arm icon representing agent-driven implementation. Adapts behavior based on operator capability: Socratic mode (agent teaches), Restricted mode (agent tests), Standard mode (agent implements). Bounded by magnitude limits (LP3). BOTTOM TIER: Reconstruction Plane (model-update layer) - shows a knowledge/theory icon. An arrow labeled 'Reconstruction Proof (RP)' flows UPWARD from this tier back toward the human operator. Contains the Design Rationale and Prediction Probes. Serves inbound theory preservation. Show the THREE INVARIANTS as labels: Outbound Intent Fidelity (Directive), Inbound Theory Preservation (Reconstruction), Operator Capability Integrity (cross-cutting across all three). The human operator sits ABOVE the Directive Plane, providing intent and receiving reconstructed theory. Use clean professional technology style.

iterations.critique:
SCORE: 8.5

STRENGTHS:
- **Excellent Information Density:** The graphic successfully maps every complex requirement from the prompt into a single cohesive visual, maintaining high structural integrity.
- **Strong Visual Hierarchy:** The use of the three distinct colors (Blue, Orange, Teal) for the pyramid tiers creates a very clear mental model for the viewer.
- **Consistent Iconography:** The icons used (brain, robotic arm, book/theory) are professionally rendered and directly support the conceptual meaning of each plane.

ISSUES:
- **Arrow Congestion:** The right side of the diagram is slightly cluttered. The "Intent Contract (IC)" arrow overlaps the pyramid edges, and the "Reconstruction Proof (RP)" arrow is fragmented into two separate visual paths, which might confuse the flow of "upward" movement.
- **Text Alignment:** Some of the secondary text (e.g., under "Outbound Intent Fidelity") is very close to icons/borders, reducing white space breathing room.

SPECIFIC_IMPROVEMENTS:
- **Streamline the RP Arrow:** Combine the two teal arrows on the right into a single, continuous sweeping upward arc that starts at the Reconstruction Plane and points directly back to the Human Operator icon to better illustrate the feedback loop.
- **Clarify the IC Arrow:** Adjust the "Intent Contract (IC)" arrow so it doesn't obscure the boundary between the Directive and Execution planes; perhaps move the handshake icon slightly to the right.
- **Enhance the Cross-Cutting Label:** The vertical text "Operator Capability Integrity" on the far right is well-placed but could benefit from a subtle background bracket or shaded pillar to better emphasize its "cross-cutting" nature across all three tiers.

VERDICT: ACCEPTABLE
